package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for same_struct_used_for_bind_persist_and_respond.
// Anti-pattern: A single struct is reused for request binding, database
// persistence, and API response serialization.

type Product struct {
	ID          uint    `json:"id" gorm:"primaryKey" form:"id" uri:"id"`
	Name        string  `json:"name" gorm:"column:name" binding:"required"`
	Description string  `json:"description" gorm:"column:description"`
	Price       float64 `json:"price" gorm:"column:price" binding:"required"`
	CategoryID  uint    `json:"category_id" gorm:"column:category_id"`
	CreatedAt   string  `json:"created_at" gorm:"column:created_at"`
	UpdatedAt   string  `json:"updated_at" gorm:"column:updated_at"`
}

func CreateProductHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var product Product

	// Anti-pattern: same struct used for binding incoming request
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&product); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: same struct used for persisting to database
	if err := db.Create(&product).Error; err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "failed to create product"})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: same struct used for responding to client
	// This leaks internal fields (CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, gorm tags) into the API
	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, product)
}

func GetProductHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var product Product

	// Anti-pattern: same struct used for database query
	if err := db.Where("id = ?", c.Param("id")).First(&product).Error; err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "product not found"})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: same struct used for response, exposing persistence details
	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, product)
}
